title: tmuxcc client → server-proxy messages
description: All control-plane messages sent by the client to the server-proxy. The client uses the single well-known broker socket to discover sessions, claim session ids, issue lifecycle commands, and — on a data connection — bind to a session with session.attach (D5, tc-4b6k.4).

SessionClaimCommand.description: Claim a named session and return its stable sessionId (D5, tc-4b6k.4). Creates the session if it does not exist and ensures its session-proxy is running. Per-name atomic: concurrent claims are serialized. The response carries { sessionId, name, created } — NO endpoint; the client binds a connection to the session with session.attach. The created flag indicates whether this claim minted the tmux session.

template.description: Optional concrete session template to apply (tc-gjdx). Applied by the driver EXACTLY ONCE, iff this claim minted the session (created:true — the tc-3y8.2 exactly-once contract); ignored when attaching to a pre-existing session. Fully-substituted (tc-gjdx.6); driver-side application is tc-gjdx.3.

shellCommand.description: Shell command to run in the initial pane of the created session. Passed as a trailing argument to new-session (symmetric with open-window/split-pane). Inert when the session already exists and this claim attaches rather than creates. tc-s5f7.2: set for bash/fish SI only when default-shell matches. Additive optional.

SessionCreateCommand.description: Create a brand-new session with the given name. Fails (session.name-taken) if a session with that name already exists.

template.description: Optional concrete session template to apply on creation (tc-gjdx). Applied iff created:true; since session.create always mints (or fails), this applies whenever the command succeeds. Fully-substituted; driver-side application is tc-gjdx.3.

env.description: Environment variables to inject into the session's initial pane. Each key-value pair is compiled to a repeated -e NAME=value flag on new-session. Requires tmux >= 3.2 (newSessionEnvFlag); an env-carrying request against an older tmux fails loud. Additive optional. tc-gjdx.2.

shellCommand.description: Shell command to run in the initial pane of the created session. Passed as a trailing argument to new-session (symmetric with open-window/split-pane). tc-s5f7.2: set for bash/fish SI only when default-shell matches. Additive optional.

SessionCreateUniqueCommand.description: Create a session with a broker-minted unique name derived from baseName (tc-295a.5 / W1.4). The broker uniquifies against its live session table (never a client-side registry) and never silently attaches to an existing session — the response always has created=true and carries the minted name.

workspaceUri.description: Optional workspace identity (S4 / tc-76m8.6). When present the broker sets it on the minted session as the @tmuxcc-workspace user-option so a later reopen matches by identity. Canonical workspace URI; omitted for folderless windows and older extensions.

template.description: Optional concrete session template to apply on creation (tc-gjdx). createUnique always mints (created:true), so this applies whenever the command succeeds. Fully-substituted; driver-side application is tc-gjdx.3.

env.description: Environment variables to inject into the session's initial pane. Each key-value pair is compiled to a repeated -e NAME=value flag on new-session. Requires tmux >= 3.2 (newSessionEnvFlag); an env-carrying request against an older tmux fails loud. Additive optional. tc-gjdx.2.

shellCommand.description: Shell command to run in the initial pane of the created session. Passed as a trailing argument to new-session (symmetric with open-window/split-pane). tc-s5f7.2: set for bash/fish SI only when default-shell matches. Additive optional.

SessionApplyTemplateCommand.description: Apply a concrete session template to a LIVE session, safe direction (tc-gjdx.4). The driver diffs the template against the live session model and creates only the windows whose names are absent live; name-matching windows are left alone (idempotent — a re-apply is a no-op). With dryRun the driver computes but does NOT create the would-create set (a preview for the confirmation UI, tc-gjdx.7). The response payload (applyTemplate) carries the would-create set (dryRun) / did-create set (real apply). A real apply executes via the same transaction machinery as apply-at-create and fails loud with no rollback (tc-gjdx.3 partial-failure semantics).

dryRun.description: True: compute the would-create set and return it WITHOUT applying (preview). False/omitted: actually create the missing windows/panes.

SessionFreezeTemplateCommand.description: Freeze a LIVE session into a schema-valid session template (tc-gjdx.5) — 'save current session as template'. The driver captures window names, per-pane working directories, and geometry (from window_layout) as a desired-geometry tree. Any client may invoke it. The response payload (frozenTemplate) is a SessionTemplate that re-applies with full topology/geometry fidelity.

name.description: Optional name to embed as the frozen template's `name`. Omitted: the driver leaves the output template unnamed (or defaults to the session name — tc-gjdx.5's call).

SessionDestroyCommand.description: Destroy an existing session and reap its session-proxy.

SessionAttachMessage.description: Bind THIS connection to a session's stream (D5, tc-4b6k.4). Sent ONCE after the server-proxy.capabilities handshake on a connection the client wants to become a DATA connection. A top-level client→server-proxy message (NOT a command.request): it transitions the connection rather than returning a correlated response. The broker ensures the session-proxy is running, detaches the connection from the command plane, and hands the transport to the session-proxy — whose snapshot + deltas continue the SAME per-connection seq counter. No second handshake, no per-session socket. Success = the session snapshot arrives; an unknown sessionId yields an error + close. flags carries the reserved D4/D8 tmux-parity client flags (ignore-size/read-only; not acted on in tc-4b6k.4). primaryPaneId is the targeted-attach hint (formerly the pane.attach command, tc-7xv.36).

ServerProxyInfoCommand.description: Read-only server-proxy diagnostics snapshot (tc-k6v). No side-effects. Older server-proxies respond with protocol.unknown-message.

ServerProxySetMetricsHttpCommand.description: Runtime toggle of the server-proxy's /metrics (+ /info) HTTP exposition (tc-44u4.4). Flip the Prometheus scrape surface on or off WITHOUT restarting the server-proxy. bind selects where the HTTP listener binds when enabled is true: "unix" (or omitted) = per-user unix-domain socket at <runtime>/<socketName>/metrics-http.sock (secure default); "unix:/abs/path.sock" = unix-domain at explicit path; "127.0.0.1:<port>" = loopback TCP (not per-user isolated). When enabled is false the listener is unbound and bind is ignored. Additive — older server-proxies respond with protocol.unknown-message.

enabled.description: Bind a listener (true) or unbind the current one (false).

bind.description: Bind address when enabled is true. Omit for the secure unix-socket default. Ignored when enabled is false.

SessionTopologyCommand.description: One-shot topology query for a discovered-but-unclaimed session (tc-i9aq.2). Runs tmux list-windows + list-panes for the named session without claiming it or spawning a session-proxy. The response carries the full window/pane topology including @tmuxcc-* fields so the extension can render unbound nodes with their durable intent. Read-only; nothing is mutated. Additive — older server-proxies respond with protocol.unknown-message.

ServerProxyCommand.description: Discriminated union of all server-proxy commands a client may issue via command.request. Narrow on `kind`. (session.attach is a top-level message, not a command — see SessionAttachMessage.)

ServerProxyCommandRequestMessage.description: Client issues a server-proxy-level command. direction: client→server-proxy.

correlationId.description: Client-generated opaque string, echoed in the matching response.

ServerProxyClientMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the client sends to the server-proxy. Note: ClientCapabilitiesMessage is shared between both wires. session.attach (D5) transitions a data connection into the session-proxy stream.
